title: SSH
description: Secure Shell protocol for encrypted remote access, tunneling, and file transfer.

entries.label: Connect to remote host
entries.description: Opens an encrypted shell session on a remote machine. Port 22 by default.

entries.label: Generate SSH key pair
entries.description: Creates a new Ed25519 key pair. Preferred over RSA for security and performance.

entries.label: Copy public key to server
entries.description: Installs your public key into the remote authorized_keys for passwordless login.

entries.label: Local port forwarding
entries.description: Forwards local port 8080 to remote port 80. Access remote services as if local.

entries.label: Copy file to remote
entries.description: Securely copies a file to a remote host over SSH. Use -r for directories.

entries.label: SSH config file
entries.command:
Host alias
  HostName example.com
  User deploy
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
entries.description: Define host aliases in ~/.ssh/config for quick connection with custom settings.

entries.label: Start SSH agent
entries.description: Starts the agent and loads your private key. Avoids repeated passphrase prompts.

entries.label: Proxy jump through bastion
entries.description: Connects to a target host through a jump server/bastion. No config file needed.

entries.label: Remote port forwarding
entries.description: Exposes your local port 3000 on the remote host's port 9090.

entries.label: Remove stale host key
entries.description: Removes a host's entry from known_hosts after a key change or reinstall.
